What a surprise, and what an honour! Number 7 in the #DataIQ100 Top 10 for 2026 is Sarah Barr Miller , Director of Data and AI, British Airways.
...
Sarah operates in one of the most data-rich and operationally complex industries in the world. Where safety, customer experience and performance depend on getting decisions right. 

Starting in hands-on data roles, she chose to move closer to decision-making — stepping into leadership roles that influence outcomes.Spanning global retail analytics and international expansion, Sarah understands how data can transform customer experience at scale. 

Today, she brings that perspective into aviation, leading data, insight and AI across a global airline, leading teams using advanced analytics and AI to optimise aircraft deployment, crew planning and maintenance prediction. Sarah believes the future of data leadership will be as much about organisational design and human outcomes as technology — blending technical credibility with communication, influence and trust.

results.title: TBS Sales Planning Analyst | Wfh
highlights:
# TBS Sales Planning Analyst
...
Molson Coors Beverage Company is transforming the beverage industry with a diverse portfolio and a commitment to innovation. The Sales Planning Analyst will manage and optimize supply and in-store conditions for retail locations in Ontario, collaborating with sales and distribution teams to achieve financial and volumetric targets while unlocking growth opportunities.
...
- Work with fellow Sales team members, supply planning, demand planning and customer contacts to delivery district sales and share performance targets
- Leverage business intelligence, customer programming and promotional plans to optimize customer ordering and stock levels
- Employ advanced analytics, modelling and regression techniques to optimize sales in a regulated, constraint driven sales environment
- Develop insights & recommendations to improve sales performance in collaboration with sales, revenue management and customer investment teams
...
- University Degree in Math, Computer Science, Engineering, or Finance
- 1-5 years of previous work experience in Advanced Analytics, Sales, Customer Service or Distribution and Logistics Experience
- Superior excel modelling skills
- Knowledge and understanding of predictive modelling, computer programming, big data/AI principles, tools & technologies

results.title: Ronak Shah
highlights:
Assistant General Counsel - Privacy & Technology at [Molson Coors Beverage Company](https://www.linkedin.com/company/molson-coors)
...
Ronak currently serves as Assistant General Counsel for Privacy & Technology at Molson Coors, where he leads the development and implementation of the company's global privacy and data protection strategy. He oversees the legal aspects of Molson Coors' cyber incident response program, guiding the company through security incidents and regulatory notification requirements. As co-chair of Molson Coors' AI Steering Committee, he also spearheads the development of enterprise-wide AI governance frameworks, policies, and standards. Appointed to the Illinois State Bar Association Standing Committee on Artificial Intelligence & the Practice of Law, and currently serve as Co-Chair of the AI, Data Privacy, and Cybersecurity Committee for the Chicago Bar Association. I am CIPM and CIPP/US certified by the International Association of Privacy Professionals, and was selected as a Foundry Fellow by the Internet Law and Policy Foundry. I received my J.D. with honors from the University of Wisconsin Law School, and am licensed in Illinois and Wisconsin.
...

results.title: Bernardo Mota
highlights:
Principal Data Scientist | Computer Vision & AI Specialist | Generative AI, ML at Scale
...
Principal Data Scientist with 7+ years of experience building and deploying AI and Machine Learning products at scale, with a strong focus on Computer Vision, Deep Learning, and Generative AI. Currently at Anheuser-Busch InBev, developing end-to-end AI solutions that interpret millions of images captured daily across multiple countries, supporting real-time decision-making and operational efficiency. I have contributed to scaling Computer Vision and personalization solutions from zero to 13 countries in less than a year, supporting business units and 4 partners, processing 35M+ images, and impacting thousands of BDRs. Previously, I was part of the Digital Insights team at Ambev Tech, helping build the Task Bees Force operation from scratch. During this time, I developed COINS and task-effectiveness ML models that scaled the product from zero to 115M+ tasks across 22K instruction types, reaching 6K+ BDRs and 3.5M+ execution hours. I also helped launch sponsored tasks, creating a new monetization stream for partner brands. Technical expertise includes Python, PySpark, SQL, Azure Databricks, Deep Learning, CNNs, Vision Transformers, Computer Vision, Recommender Systems, NLP, Generative AI, MLOps, Model Validation, and Experimentation. Strong background in Statistics and experimental design, with experience connecting advanced AI models to real business impact. Comfortable working with both technical and non-technical stakeholders. 🎓 MSc in Data Science – University of São Paulo (USP) ✍️ Writing about AI & Data Science on Medium:
...
Lead the design, training, deployment, and monitoring of Computer Vision models at scale, supporting AI products operating across multiple countries and partners. Drive the development of multimodal GenAI solutions for price recognition and advanced image understanding, connecting model innovation to real-time business execution. Define strategies for dataset scalability and robustness, including 3D synthetic data generation, fraud detection, and validation frameworks to improve quality and compliance. Optimize labeling efficiency and model performance through ILP-based sample selection and scalable ML standards, reusable frameworks, and lifecycle best practices. Drive architectural decisions across squads, balancing scalability, cost, reliability, and maintainability for global AI solutions.
...
Designed, trained, and deployed Computer Vision models for classification, detection, and image understanding, helping process 35M+ images across 13 countries. Joined the Computer Vision team at the start of the Vision 2.0 journey, contributing to solutions in detection, scene classification, product and trade material recognition, fraud detection, and price recognition. Helped expand the operation from Brazil to 13 countries in less than a year, supporting business units, 4 partners, and thousands of BDRs. Delivered end-to-end AI systems, from data ingestion and training to real-time inference and monitoring, using Deep Learning and multimodal Generative AI approaches. Improved model robustness and operational efficiency through synthetic data generation, optimized image selection for labeling, and production-grade validation pipelines.
...
in the beer and brewing
...
development of scalable AI/ML products. Developed COINS and task-effectiveness ML models that scaled the product from zero to 115M+ tasks across 22K instruction types, reaching 6K+ BDRs and 3.5M+ execution hours. Helped launch sponsored tasks, creating a new monetization stream for partner brands while strengthening execution strategy and increasing volume and distribution. Built and deployed scalable ML pipelines using Python, PySpark, and Azure Databricks for training, validation, and production deployment. Contributed to the Data Science Guild, promoting best practices, technical standards, and knowledge sharing across the company.
